title: akoúō Claim Taxonomy
description: Shared claim taxonomy for separating heard, measured, inferred, interpreted, speculative, and undetermined claims.

heard.description: Claims directly present in the audio, prompt, transcript, field note, or supplied description.

measured.description: Claims produced by file, signal, waveform, spectrogram, metadata, or other technical inspection.

inferred.description: Plausible logical, technical, or forensic deductions that are not directly confirmed by available evidence. Do not place cultural theory, affective readings, or symbolic interpretation here.

interpreted.description: Cultural, theoretical, affective, ecological, archival, political, or contextual readings.

speculative.description: Declared fictional, symbolic, imaginative, mythic, or possible-world readings.

undetermined.description: Claims that cannot be responsibly made from the available input.

basis.description: Short note describing what supports or limits the claim.
